todaysEvents.summary: Gulf leaders meet Jeddah - first GCC summit since war began; Saudi, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, UAE attend to discuss Iranian strikes and diplomatic path (Al Jazeera, Middle East Eye, Apr 28).
todaysEvents.delta: Mild de-escalation: First coordinated Gulf diplomatic meeting signals regional solidarity; may moderate IRGC pressure on GCC states

todaysEvents.summary: May 1 War Powers deadline - Congress must authorize Iran war by May 1 (60 days from Feb 28); Democrats may sue if Trump continues without AUMF; Senate rejected curbs 52-47 (Military.com, Time, Al Jazeera, Apr 24-28).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalatory risk: Congressional constraint on Trump could weaken deterrence; Iran may interpret war powers conflict as opening for escalation

todaysEvents.summary: Brent confirmed $111.16 Apr 28 (+2.7%, TradingEconomics); Iran 'still in war situation'; Trump reviews Hormuz proposal with aides; Hormuz 13 transits/day; ceasefire holds; tail 45% (Apr 29).
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Market raised risk premium despite Rubio rejection (D57 est. ~$102 was wrong); structural disruption unchanged; Iran proposal still on table

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: GCC Jeddah summit (Apr 28) and Trump review of Iran proposal provide narrow path - congressional AUMF passes before May 1 and Iran accepts modified framework with nuclear addendum; GCC states facilitate; vessels returned; Hormuz reopens. Narrow (10%) - May 1 deadline creates legal uncertainty; Iran 'still in war situation'; nuclear precondition remains; mine-clearing ~6 months regardless of deal (PBS); full commercial reopening delayed. Brent retreats toward $92-98/bbl on deal; iTraxx tightens toward 175bp.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: May 1 War Powers deadline navigated via AUMF vote or presidential defiance; nuclear stalemate persists; US blockade and IRGC Hormuz enforcement co-exist; selective bilateral transit; periodic vessel seizures. GCC Jeddah summit stabilises Gulf states but does not unlock breakthrough. Brent stabilises $100-115/bbl; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 195-215bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026. Dominant scenario (45%).

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: May 1 War Powers deadline triggers political crisis - Congress blocks AUMF or Democrats sue; Iran interprets US domestic constraint as operational window; IRGC ROE hardens; 'shoot and kill' order triggers incident with IRGC small boat. Gulf-wide blockade activated; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; Malaysia June gap becomes July-August crisis. Market already pricing tail risk in Brent $111 despite Rubio rejection (Apr 28). Tail 45%.
